folio.label: 35r
folio.l: Ou©t of his toun , a iourne largely
And hom went euäy man , the rigút wey
There was nomore , but fare wel haue good day
Of this bataille , I wol nomore endyte
But speke of Palamon , and of Arcite
Swelleth the brest of Arcite , and the sore
Encresetú , at his hert , more and more
The clotered blod , for ony lechecraft
Coruptetú , and is in his bouke I laft´
That neither veyn blod , ne ventusyng
Ne drynk of herbes , may ben his helpyng
The vertue of explyf , or AnimaÂ
For thilk vätue , cleped naturaÂ
Ne may the venym voide , ne expelle
The pipes of his longen , gonen swelle
And euäy lacerte , in his brest a doun
Nature hatú now , no dominaciou~
And certeynly , there nature wyl nat werche
Fare wel Phisyk , go bere æe man to cherche
This is al and som , that Arcite mot deye
For whicú he sendeth , after Emelye
And Palamou~ , that was his Cosyn dere
Than seide he thus , as ©e shuln after here
¢ Nat may the wooful spirit , in myn hert
Declare a poynt , of alle my sorwes smert
To ©ow my lady , that I loue most
But I quethe , the , seruyse of my gost
To ©ow , a bouen euäy creature
Sen that my lif , may no lengere dure
Allas the woo , allas the peynes stronge

note.notetxt: 1911 There is a paragraph mark and a No bn~ in the left margin.Est

folio.l: That I for ©ow haue , suffred , and so longe
Allas the deth , allas myn Emelye
Allas de¹tyng , of oure companye
Allas myn hertes Quene , allas my wyf´
Myn hertes lady , endere of my lif
What is this world , what asken men to haue
Now with his loue , now in his cold gue
¢ I haue here with my Cosyn , Palamou~
Had strif and rancour , many a day a gou~
For loue of ©ow , and for my Ielousie
And Iubiter , so wysly , my soule gye
To speken of a serunt , ¸prely
W circumstauncez , aÂ trewely
That is to seyn , trewæe , honoå , kny©thede
Wysxxxx humblesse , estate , and heigh kynrede
